message:
feat(quic): strengthen config validation and connection tracking

Config improvements:
- Add is_valid_for_server() validation ensuring cert_chain/private_key
  are present when client_auth is Optional/Required
- Cap initial_window_size to RFC 7540 maximum (2^31-1) to prevent
  flow control overflow
- Change endpoint client() to take &QuicConfig reference instead of
  owned value, reducing unnecessary cloning

Connection improvements:
- Fix is_open() to also check inner.close_reason() alongside tracker
  state, ensuring accurate open/closed detection when connection
  closes externally without tracker notification
- Add max_datagram_size() and datagram send/receive methods for
  unreliable data transfer

Stream improvements:
- Export StreamTracker from module for use in integration tests
- Ensure tracker is properly marked closing on connection drop

Error handling:
- Add Datagram variant to QuicError for datagram-specific failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,10 +19,14 @@ pub enum QuicError {
     #[error("write error: {0}")]
     Write(#[from] quinn::WriteError),
 
-    /// Error reading from a stream.
+    /// Error reading from a stream (exact read).
     #[error("read error: {0}")]
     Read(#[from] quinn::ReadExactError),
 
+    /// Error reading from a stream.
+    #[error("read error: {0}")]
+    ReadStream(#[from] quinn::ReadError),
+
     /// Stream read finished unexpectedly.
     #[error("read to end error: {0}")]
     ReadToEnd(#[from] quinn::ReadToEndError),
@@ -70,7 +74,14 @@ impl QuicError {
     /// Check if this error is recoverable (connection can continue).
     #[must_use]
     pub fn is_recoverable(&self) -> bool {
-        matches!(self, Self::StreamClosed | Self::Read(_) | Self::Write(_))
+        matches!(
+            self,
+            Self::StreamClosed
+                | Self::Read(_)
+                | Self::ReadStream(_)
+                | Self::ReadToEnd(_)
+                | Self::Write(_)
+        )
     }
 
     /// Check if this is a connection-level error.
